raw_consumer_statement:
Summary : I am submitting this complaint against Citibank regarding its handling of a billing dispute I filed on XX/XX/year>, for a furniture purchase ( over {$2700.00} ) made through the XXXX platform. The merchandise was damaged and not the correct color/finish I ordered. Despite my efforts to resolve the issue with the seller and through XXXX advertised XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX, I was forced to dispute the charge with Citi when both parties refused to cooperate. 

XXXX XXXX : I submitted photographic and written evidence via email to XXXX XXXX XXXX Department, as instructed by their representative. 
I was not informed that there were any file format requirements or that Citi might be unable to view photos from a phone. In fact, I asked whether I should mail hard copies, and I was told email was sufficient. 
Citi never contacted me to request different formats or additional information. 
I was only informed by a Citi supervisor ( XXXX, XXXX ) during a follow-up call on XX/XX/year>, that the evidence I sent could not be viewed. XXXX stated that Citi " doesnt have time to contact cardmembers about every little issue. '' As a result, Citi reversed my conditional credit without reviewing my evidence and closed the case in favor of the merchant. 
This is unacceptable and represents a failure to provide a fair and transparent dispute resolution process. I made a good faith effort to follow Citis instructions, but Citi failed to notify me that my documentation was unreadable or to give me a chance to resubmit in a proper format. 

Attempts to Resolve : I emailed Citi again on XX/XX/XXXX requesting the dispute be reopened with proper review of my materials. 
I called that same day and spoke to a supervisor who could not confirm whether the case would be reviewed and indicated I should not expect a different outcome. 
She also said the only resolution would be through XXXX or the seller, despite the dispute being with my credit card company, not the platform.
